title: About
text:
This site is a personal data journalism project focused on college and
      professional basketball. Every post is built from raw game-by-game data —
      no box-score summaries, no recycled narratives. Just the data, visualized
      and interpreted.
    
    
      A basketball fan who believes the best stories in sports are hiding in the
      data. Built this site to share those stories publicly.

title: About
text:
The Stack







Tool
Purpose




R
Data ingestion, wrangling, and visualization (tidyverse, ggplot2)


Quarto
Rendering and publishing


GitHub Pages
Hosting


ESPN API
Game-by-game data source





Questions, feedback, or data requests? Reach out on GitHub.

title: Classroom to Court
text:
Basketball has always been more than a game — it’s a laboratory of strategy, athleticism, and storytelling. Classroom to Court bridges the gap between the academic study of sport and the hardwood itself, turning raw statistics into narratives that every fan can appreciate.
Whether you follow the high-stakes drama of March Madness or track every NBA standings shift across an 82-game season, this site gives you something most coverage doesn’t: the numbers behind the moments. Every chart, every breakdown, and every post here is built from real game-by-game data — no filler, no recycled takes, just basketball and the statistics that explain it.

title: Matchups
text:
These reports compare two teams or two players side by side — works across NBA, NCAA, NFL, NHL, and MLB. Open the .qmd source, set both sides of the matchup in the settings block at the top, and re-render.

Team Matchup Report
Player Matchup Report

title: Player Matchup Report
text:
# install.packages("nflreadr")
# install.packages("baseballr")
# pak::pak("sportsdataverse/hoopR")
# pak::pak("sportsdataverse/hockeyR")
# install.packages("dplyr")
# install.packages("ggplot2")
# install.packages("tidyr")

library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(tidyr)

title: Player Matchup Report
section: ⚙️ Your Settings — Change These Before Rendering
text:
⚙️ Your Settings — Change These Before Rendering

# ============================================================
#  FILL IN YOUR INFO BELOW — this is the only section
#  you need to change each time you run this report.
# ============================================================

# Which sport?
# Choose ONE of: "NBA"  |  "NCAA"  |  "NFL"  |  "NHL"  |  "MLB"
my_sport <- "NBA"

# Player 1 full name (exactly as the data source lists it)
player_1 <- "LeBron James"

# Player 2 full name
player_2 <- "Stephen Curry"

# Position / type — used to compare the right stats.
# NBA/NCAA: "Guard"  |  "Wing/Forward"  |  "Big/Center"
# NFL:      "QB"     |  "RB"            |  "WR"          | "TE"
# NHL:      "Skater" |  "Goalie"
# MLB:      "Batter" |  "Pitcher"
player_type <- "Wing/Forward"

# Which season?
# NBA/NCAA: year season ENDS  (2025-26 = 2026)
# NFL: year season STARTS     (2024-25 = 2024)
# NHL: full string            ("20242025")
# MLB: calendar year          (2024)
season_year <- 2026

# Season label for display only
season_label <- "2025-26"

# ============================================================
#  THAT'S IT — scroll down to render and read your report.
# ============================================================

title: Player Matchup Report
text:
Side-by-Side Chart

ggplot(chart_df, aes(x = Stat, y = Value, fill = Player)) +
  geom_col(position = "dodge", width = 0.65) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("#3498db","#e74c3c")) +
  labs(
    title    = paste(player_1, "vs.", player_2),
    subtitle = paste(my_sport, season_label, "|", player_type),
    x        = NULL, y = "Value", fill = NULL
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(legend.position = "top",
        axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 30, hjust = 1))









adv_df <- data.frame(
  Stat       = stat_names,
  Advantage  = advantage
) %>%
  filter(Advantage != "N/A") %>%
  mutate(
    Winner = factor(Advantage, levels = c(player_1, "Even", player_2))
  )

ggplot(adv_df, aes(x = Stat, fill = Winner)) +
  geom_bar(width = 0.6) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = setNames(c("#3498db","#95a5a6","#e74c3c"),
                                       c(player_1,"Even",player_2))) +
  labs(
    title    = "Category Edge Summary",
    subtitle = paste(player_1, "vs.", player_2),
    x        = NULL, y = "Count", fill = NULL
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(legend.position = "top",
        axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 30, hjust = 1))

title: Team Matchup Report
section: ⚙️ Your Settings — Change These Before Rendering
text:
⚙️ Your Settings — Change These Before Rendering

# ============================================================
#  FILL IN YOUR INFO BELOW — this is the only section
#  you need to change each time you run this report.
# ============================================================

# Which sport?
# Choose ONE of: "NBA"  |  "NCAA"  |  "NFL"  |  "NHL"  |  "MLB"
my_sport <- "NBA"

# Team 1 name or abbreviation (same format as the grade reports for this sport)
# NBA/NCAA: use location name  e.g. "Boston", "Duke"
# NFL: abbreviation            e.g. "KC", "BUF"
# NHL: abbreviation            e.g. "BOS", "EDM"
# MLB: abbreviation            e.g. "NYY", "LAD"
team_1 <- "Boston"

# Team 2 name or abbreviation
team_2 <- "Golden State"

# Which season? Same convention as the grade reports for this sport:
# NBA/NCAA: year season ENDS  (2025-26 = 2026)
# NFL: year season STARTS     (2024-25 = 2024)
# NHL: year season STARTS     (2024-25 = 2024)
# MLB: calendar year          (2024 = 2024)
season_year <- 2026

# Season label for display only
season_label <- "2025-26"

# ============================================================
#  THAT'S IT — scroll down to render and read your report.
# ============================================================

title: Team Matchup Report
text:
Side-by-Side Chart

ggplot(chart_data, aes(x = Stat, y = Value, fill = Team)) +
  geom_col(position = "dodge", width = 0.65) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("#3498db","#e74c3c")) +
  geom_text(aes(label = sprintf("%.1f", Value)),
            position = position_dodge(width = 0.65), vjust = -0.4, size = 3.2) +
  labs(
    title    = paste(team_1, "vs.", team_2, "— Season Averages"),
    subtitle = paste(my_sport, season_label),
    x        = NULL,
    y        = "Value",
    fill     = NULL
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(legend.position = "top",
        axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 25, hjust = 1))

title: Team Matchup Report
text:
Scoring Trends Side by Side

pts_label_short <- switch(my_sport, "MLB"="Runs","NHL"="Goals","Points")

trend1 <- d1 %>% mutate(game_num=row_number(), team=team_1)
trend2 <- d2 %>% mutate(game_num=row_number(), team=team_2)

bind_rows(trend1, trend2) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x=game_num, y=pts, color=team)) +
  geom_smooth(se=FALSE, linewidth=1.2) +
  geom_point(alpha=0.3, size=1.5) +
  scale_color_manual(values=c("#3498db","#e74c3c")) +
  labs(
    title    = paste(team_1, "vs.", team_2, "—", pts_label_short, "Scored (Smoothed Trend)"),
    subtitle = paste(my_sport, season_label),
    x        = "Game Number", y = pts_label_short, color = NULL
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size=13) +
  theme(legend.position="top")

title: Team Matchup Report
text:
Advantage Summary

adv_df <- data.frame(
  Category  = c(pts_label, opp_label, diff_label, "Win %"),
  Team_1    = c(s1$pts, s1$opp_pts, s1$pt_diff, s1$win_pct),
  Team_2    = c(s2$pts, s2$opp_pts, s2$pt_diff, s2$win_pct),
  Lower_Better = c(FALSE, TRUE, FALSE, FALSE)
) %>%
  mutate(
    Advantage = case_when(
      !Lower_Better & Team_1 > Team_2 ~ team_1,
      !Lower_Better & Team_2 > Team_1 ~ team_2,
      Lower_Better  & Team_1 < Team_2 ~ team_1,
      Lower_Better  & Team_2 < Team_1 ~ team_2,
      TRUE ~ "Even"
    )
  ) %>%
  select(Category, Advantage)

t1_adv <- sum(adv_df$Advantage == team_1)
t2_adv <- sum(adv_df$Advantage == team_2)

knitr::kable(adv_df, col.names = c("Category","Advantage"))




Category
Advantage




Points Per Game
Golden State


Points Allowed/G
Boston


Point Differential
Boston


Win %
Boston





Advantages: Boston wins 3 of 4 categories | Golden State wins 1 of 4 categories

title: NBA Player Season Grade Report
text:
library(hoopR)
library(dplyr)
library(readr)
library(ggplot2)
library(tidyr)

title: NBA Player Season Grade Report
section: ⚙️ Your Settings — Change These Before Rendering
text:
⚙️ Your Settings — Change These Before Rendering

# ============================================================
#  FILL IN YOUR INFO BELOW — this is the only section
#  you need to change each time you run this report.
# ============================================================

# Which NBA player do you want to grade?
# Use the player's full name exactly as ESPN lists it (First Last).
# Examples: "LeBron James", "Stephen Curry", "Nikola Jokic"
my_player <- "LeBron James"

# Which season? Use the year the season ENDS.
# Example: the 2025-26 NBA season is written as 2026.
season_year <- 2026

# Season label shown in the report (for display only)
season_label <- "2025-26"

# Player position — used to set rebounding and assist benchmarks.
# Choose ONE of: "Guard"  |  "Wing/Forward"  |  "Big/Center"
player_position <- "Wing/Forward"

# ============================================================
#  THAT'S IT — scroll down to render and read your report.
# ============================================================

title: NBA Player Season Grade Report
text:
LeBron James — Los Angeles Lakers | 2025-26
Position: Wing/Forward | Games Graded: 73 | Minutes Per Game: 32.8
Overall Season Grade: B+

title: NBA Player Season Grade Report
text:
Category Grades

grade_colors <- c("A"="#2ecc71","B"="#3498db","C"="#f39c12","D"="#e67e22","F"="#e74c3c")

ggplot(grade_df, aes(x = reorder(Category, GPA), y = GPA, fill = Grade_Group)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.7) +
  geom_text(aes(label = Grade), hjust = -0.2, fontface = "bold", size = 4.5) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = grade_colors, name = "Grade") +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 5.2), breaks = c(0,1,2,3,4)) +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(
    title    = paste(player_name_full, "— Category Grades"),
    subtitle = paste(season_label, "NBA Season |", player_position),
    x        = NULL,
    y        = "Grade Points (4.0 scale)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(legend.position = "right")

title: NBA Player Season Grade Report
text:
Scoring Trend

trend_data <- player_data %>%
  arrange(game_date) %>%
  mutate(game_num = row_number())

ggplot(trend_data, aes(x = game_num, y = pts)) +
  geom_line(color = "#3498db", linewidth = 1) +
  geom_point(color = "#3498db", size = 2) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = avg$pts, linetype = "dashed", color = "#e74c3c", linewidth = 0.8) +
  annotate("text", x = max(trend_data$game_num), y = avg$pts + 1,
           label = paste("Avg:", sprintf("%.1f", avg$pts)), color = "#e74c3c", hjust = 1, size = 3.5) +
  labs(
    title    = paste(player_name_full, "— Points Per Game"),
    subtitle = "Red dashed line = season average",
    x        = "Game Number",
    y        = "Points"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13)

title: NBA Player Season Grade Report
text:
Game-by-Game Stats: Points, Rebounds, Assists

pra_long <- trend_data %>%
  select(game_num, pts, reb, ast) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = c(pts, reb, ast), names_to = "Stat", values_to = "Value") %>%
  mutate(Stat = recode(Stat, pts="Points", reb="Rebounds", ast="Assists"))

ggplot(pra_long, aes(x = game_num, y = Value, color = Stat)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.9) +
  geom_point(size = 1.8) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Points"="#3498db","Rebounds"="#2ecc71","Assists"="#e67e22")) +
  labs(
    title    = paste(player_name_full, "— Points / Rebounds / Assists by Game"),
    x        = "Game Number",
    y        = "Count",
    color    = NULL
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(legend.position = "top")

title: NBA Player Season Grade Report
text:
Shooting Splits

shooting_df <- data.frame(
  Shot_Type = c("Field Goals", "Three-Pointers", "Free Throws"),
  Pct       = c(avg$fg_pct, avg$three_pct, avg$ft_pct),
  Benchmark = c(47, 36, 78)
)

shooting_long <- shooting_df %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = c(Pct, Benchmark), names_to = "Type", values_to = "Value") %>%
  mutate(Type = recode(Type, Pct = player_name_full, Benchmark = "NBA Average"))

ggplot(shooting_long, aes(x = Shot_Type, y = Value, fill = Type)) +
  geom_col(position = "dodge", width = 0.6) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("#3498db","#bdc3c7")) +
  geom_text(aes(label = sprintf("%.1f%%", Value)),
            position = position_dodge(width = 0.6), vjust = -0.4, size = 3.5) +
  labs(
    title    = paste(player_name_full, "— Shooting Splits vs. NBA Average"),
    x        = NULL,
    y        = "Percentage",
    fill     = NULL
  ) +
  ylim(0, 105) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(legend.position = "top")

title: NBA Player Season Grade Report
text:
Key Stats Summary

summary_tbl <- data.frame(
  Statistic = c("Points Per Game","Rebounds Per Game","Assists Per Game",
                "Steals Per Game","Blocks Per Game","Turnovers Per Game",
                "Field Goal %","Three-Point %","Free Throw %","Efficiency Index"),
  Value     = c(
    sprintf("%.1f", avg$pts),
    sprintf("%.1f", avg$reb),
    sprintf("%.1f", avg$ast),
    sprintf("%.1f", avg$stl),
    sprintf("%.1f", avg$blk),
    sprintf("%.1f", avg$tov),
    sprintf("%.1f%%", avg$fg_pct),
    sprintf("%.1f%%", avg$three_pct),
    sprintf("%.1f%%", avg$ft_pct),
    sprintf("%.1f",   avg$efficiency)
  )
)

knitr::kable(summary_tbl, align = c("l","r"), col.names = c("Statistic","Season Avg"))




Statistic
Season Avg




Points Per Game
20.6


Rebounds Per Game
6.0


Assists Per Game
7.0


Steals Per Game
1.2


Blocks Per Game
0.5


Turnovers Per Game
3.0


Field Goal %
50.3%


Three-Point %
31.6%


Free Throw %
73.6%


Efficiency Index
24.7

title: NBA Player Season Grade Report
text:
How the Grade Is Calculated
Each category is graded against NBA starter-level averages from the 2024-25 season and converted to a 4.0 scale. Scoring carries the most weight (22%), followed by field goal percentage (12%), rebounding (15%), and assists (13%). Rebounding and assist benchmarks shift automatically based on the position set at the top of the file — a center is held to a higher rebounding standard than a guard.
The Efficiency Index adds together points, rebounds, assists, steals, and blocks, then subtracts turnovers and missed field goals — a simple measure of overall contribution per game.
The shooting splits chart compares the player’s percentages directly against the NBA average so you can see at a glance where they over- or under-perform.

title: NBA Team Season Grade Report
text:
library(hoopR)
library(dplyr)
library(readr)
library(ggplot2)
library(tidyr)

title: NBA Team Season Grade Report
section: ⚙️ Your Settings — Change These Before Rendering
text:
⚙️ Your Settings — Change These Before Rendering

# ============================================================
#  FILL IN YOUR INFO BELOW — this is the only section
#  you need to change each time you run this report.
# ============================================================

# Which NBA team do you want to grade?
# Use the team's common name exactly as ESPN lists it.
# Examples: "Lakers", "Celtics", "Warriors", "Knicks", "Heat"
my_team <- "Lakers"

# Which season? Use the year the season ENDS.
# Example: the 2025-26 NBA season is written as 2026.
season_year <- 2026

# Season label shown in the report (for display only)
season_label <- "2025-26"

# ============================================================
#  THAT'S IT — scroll down to render and read your report.
# ============================================================

title: NBA Team Season Grade Report
text:
Los Angeles Lakers — 2025-26 Season Report
Record: 57-35 (62.0% win rate) | Point Differential: +1.0 per game | Games Graded: 92
Overall Season Grade: B+

title: NBA Team Season Grade Report
text:
Category Grades

grade_colors <- c("A"="#2ecc71","B"="#3498db","C"="#f39c12","D"="#e67e22","F"="#e74c3c")

ggplot(grade_df, aes(x = reorder(Category, GPA), y = GPA, fill = Grade_Group)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.7) +
  geom_text(aes(label = Grade), hjust = -0.2, fontface = "bold", size = 4.5) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = grade_colors, name = "Grade") +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 5.2), breaks = c(0,1,2,3,4)) +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(
    title    = paste(team_name_full, "— Category Grades"),
    subtitle = paste(season_label, "NBA Season"),
    x        = NULL,
    y        = "Grade Points (4.0 scale)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(legend.position = "right")

title: NBA Team Season Grade Report
text:
Scoring Trend

trend_data <- team_data %>%
  mutate(
    game_date  = as.Date(game_date),
    team_score = as.numeric(team_score),
    opp_score  = as.numeric(opponent_team_score),
    result     = ifelse(team_winner == TRUE, "Win", "Loss")
  ) %>%
  arrange(game_date) %>%
  mutate(game_num = row_number())

ggplot(trend_data, aes(x = game_num)) +
  geom_line(aes(y = team_score), color = "#2ecc71", linewidth = 1) +
  geom_line(aes(y = opp_score),  color = "#e74c3c", linewidth = 1, linetype = "dashed") +
  geom_point(aes(y = team_score, color = result), size = 2) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Win"="#2ecc71","Loss"="#e74c3c"), name = "Result") +
  geom_hline(yintercept = avg$pts, linetype = "dotted", color = "#2ecc71", linewidth = 0.7) +
  labs(
    title    = paste(team_name_full, "— Scoring by Game"),
    subtitle = "Green = team score  |  Red dashed = opponent score  |  Dotted = season average",
    x        = "Game Number",
    y        = "Points"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13)

title: NBA Team Season Grade Report
text:
Win / Loss Rolling Record

trend_data <- trend_data %>%
  mutate(win_flag = ifelse(result == "Win", 1, 0),
         cumulative_wins   = cumsum(win_flag),
         cumulative_losses = cumsum(1 - win_flag))

trend_long <- trend_data %>%
  select(game_num, cumulative_wins, cumulative_losses) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = c(cumulative_wins, cumulative_losses),
               names_to = "Type", values_to = "Count") %>%
  mutate(Type = recode(Type, cumulative_wins="Wins", cumulative_losses="Losses"))

ggplot(trend_long, aes(x = game_num, y = Count, color = Type)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1.1) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Wins"="#2ecc71","Losses"="#e74c3c")) +
  labs(
    title    = paste(team_name_full, "— Cumulative Win/Loss Record"),
    x        = "Game Number",
    y        = "Games",
    color    = NULL
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(legend.position = "top")

title: NBA Team Season Grade Report
text:
Key Stats Summary

summary_tbl <- data.frame(
  Statistic = c("Points Per Game","Opp. Points Per Game","Point Differential",
                "Field Goal %","Three-Point %","Free Throw %",
                "Rebounds Per Game","Assists Per Game",

  Value     = c(
    sprintf("%.1f",   avg$pts),
    sprintf("%.1f",   avg$opp_pts),
    sprintf("%+.1f",  avg$pt_diff),
    sprintf("%.1f%%", avg$fg_pct),
    sprintf("%.1f%%", avg$three_pct),
    sprintf("%.1f%%", avg$ft_pct),
    sprintf("%.1f",   avg$reb),
    sprintf("%.1f",   avg$ast),
    sprintf("%.1f",   avg$stl),
    sprintf("%.1f",   avg$blk),
    sprintf("%.1f",   avg$tov)
  )
)

title: NBA Team Season Grade Report
text:
How the Grade Is Calculated
Each category is graded against NBA league averages from the 2024-25 season and converted to a 4.0 scale. Scoring and defense each carry 15% of the overall grade. Field goal percentage accounts for 12%, while win percentage, ball security, rebounding, and assists each account for 10%.
The grade scale is: A+ (elite) · A / A- (excellent) · B range (above average) · C range (league average) · D range (below average) · F (well below average).

title: NCAA Player Season Grade Report
text:
library(hoopR)
library(dplyr)
library(readr)
library(ggplot2)
library(tidyr)

title: NCAA Player Season Grade Report
section: ⚙️ Your Settings — Change These Before Rendering
text:
⚙️ Your Settings — Change These Before Rendering

# ============================================================
#  FILL IN YOUR INFO BELOW — this is the only section
#  you need to change each time you run this report.
# ============================================================

# Which player do you want to grade?
# Use the player's full name exactly as ESPN lists it (First Last).
# Examples: "Cooper Flagg", "Zach Edey", "Hunter Dickinson"
my_player <- "Cooper Flagg"

# Which season? Use the year the season ENDS.
# Example: the 2024-25 season is written as 2025.
season_year <- 2025

# Season label shown in the report (for display only)
season_label <- "2024-25"

# Player position — used to set rebounding and assist benchmarks.
# Choose ONE of: "Guard"  |  "Wing/Forward"  |  "Big/Center"
player_position <- "Wing/Forward"

# ============================================================
#  THAT'S IT — scroll down to render and read your report.
# ============================================================

title: NCAA Player Season Grade Report
text:
Cooper Flagg — Duke Blue Devils | 2024-25
Position: Wing/Forward | Games Graded: 37 | Minutes Per Game: 30.6
Overall Season Grade: A-

title: NCAA Player Season Grade Report
text:
Category Grades

grade_colors <- c("A"="#2ecc71","B"="#3498db","C"="#f39c12","D"="#e67e22","F"="#e74c3c")

ggplot(grade_df, aes(x = reorder(Category, GPA), y = GPA, fill = Grade_Group)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.7) +
  geom_text(aes(label = Grade), hjust = -0.2, fontface = "bold", size = 4.5) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = grade_colors, name = "Grade") +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 5.2), breaks = c(0,1,2,3,4)) +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(
    title    = paste(player_name_full, "— Category Grades"),
    subtitle = paste(season_label, "NCAA Season |", player_position),
    x        = NULL,
    y        = "Grade Points (4.0 scale)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(legend.position = "right")

title: NCAA Player Season Grade Report
text:
Scoring Trend

trend_data <- player_data %>%
  arrange(game_date) %>%
  mutate(game_num = row_number())

ggplot(trend_data, aes(x = game_num, y = pts)) +
  geom_line(color = "#3498db", linewidth = 1) +
  geom_point(color = "#3498db", size = 2.5) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = avg$pts, linetype = "dashed", color = "#e74c3c", linewidth = 0.8) +
  annotate("text", x = max(trend_data$game_num), y = avg$pts + 0.8,
           label = paste("Avg:", sprintf("%.1f", avg$pts)), color = "#e74c3c", hjust = 1, size = 3.5) +
  labs(
    title    = paste(player_name_full, "— Points Per Game"),
    subtitle = "Red dashed line = season average",
    x        = "Game Number",
    y        = "Points"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13)

title: NCAA Player Season Grade Report
text:
Game-by-Game Stats: Points, Rebounds, Assists

pra_long <- trend_data %>%
  select(game_num, pts, reb, ast) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = c(pts, reb, ast), names_to = "Stat", values_to = "Value") %>%
  mutate(Stat = recode(Stat, pts="Points", reb="Rebounds", ast="Assists"))

ggplot(pra_long, aes(x = game_num, y = Value, color = Stat)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.9) +
  geom_point(size = 2) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Points"="#3498db","Rebounds"="#2ecc71","Assists"="#e67e22")) +
  labs(
    title    = paste(player_name_full, "— Points / Rebounds / Assists by Game"),
    x        = "Game Number",
    y        = "Count",
    color    = NULL
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(legend.position = "top")

title: NCAA Player Season Grade Report
text:
Key Stats Summary

summary_tbl <- data.frame(
  Statistic = c("Points Per Game","Rebounds Per Game","Assists Per Game",
                "Steals Per Game","Blocks Per Game","Turnovers Per Game",
                "Field Goal %","Three-Point %","Free Throw %","Efficiency Index"),
  Value     = c(
    sprintf("%.1f", avg$pts),
    sprintf("%.1f", avg$reb),
    sprintf("%.1f", avg$ast),
    sprintf("%.1f", avg$stl),
    sprintf("%.1f", avg$blk),
    sprintf("%.1f", avg$tov),
    sprintf("%.1f%%", avg$fg_pct),
    sprintf("%.1f%%", avg$three_pct),
    sprintf("%.1f%%", avg$ft_pct),
    sprintf("%.1f",  avg$efficiency)
  )
)

knitr::kable(summary_tbl, align = c("l","r"), col.names = c("Statistic","Season Avg"))

Season Avg

Rebounds Per Game

4.2

title: NCAA Player Season Grade Report
text:
How the Grade Is Calculated
Each category is graded against NCAA Division I starter averages and converted to a 4.0 scale. Scoring carries the most weight (22%), followed by field goal percentage (12%), rebounding (15%), and assists (13%). Rebounding and assist benchmarks adjust automatically based on the position you set at the top of the file.
The Efficiency Index adds together points, rebounds, assists, steals, and blocks, then subtracts turnovers and missed field goals — a simple measure of overall contribution per game.

title: NCAA Team Season Grade Report
text:
Scoring Trend

trend_data <- team_data %>%
  mutate(
    game_date  = as.Date(game_date),
    team_score = as.numeric(team_score),
    opp_score  = as.numeric(opponent_team_score),
    result     = ifelse(team_winner == TRUE, "Win", "Loss")
  ) %>%
  arrange(game_date) %>%
  mutate(game_num = row_number())

ggplot(trend_data, aes(x = game_num)) +
  geom_line(aes(y = team_score), color = "#2ecc71", linewidth = 1) +
  geom_line(aes(y = opp_score),  color = "#e74c3c", linewidth = 1, linetype = "dashed") +
  geom_point(aes(y = team_score, color = result), size = 2.5) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Win"="#2ecc71","Loss"="#e74c3c"), name = "Result") +
  geom_hline(yintercept = avg$pts, linetype = "dotted", color = "#2ecc71", linewidth = 0.7) +
  labs(
    title    = paste(team_name_full, "— Scoring by Game"),
    subtitle = "Green = team score  |  Red dashed = opponent score  |  Dotted = season average",
    x        = "Game Number",
    y        = "Points"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13)

How the Grade Is Calculated
Each category is graded against NCAA Division I per-game averages and converted to a 4.0 scale. The overall grade is a weighted average of all categories, with scoring and defense carrying the most weight (15% each). Ball security and win percentage each account for 10%.
The grade scale is: A+ (elite) · A / A- (excellent) · B range (above average) · C range (average) · D range (below average) · F (well below average).

title: UNC Tar Heels
text:
Analysis of 2025-26 University of North Carolina mens basketball team

library(tidyverse)
library(readxl)
library(shiny)
library(DT)
library(shinydashboard)
library(plotly)


team_North_Carolina_2026 <- read_excel("C:/Users/Pat/Desktop/team_North_Carolina_2026.xlsx")
View(team_North_Carolina_2026)



Predict Game Outcomes

# Create dashboard layout

ui <- dashboardPage(
  
  dashboardHeader(title = "College Basketball Dashboard"),
  
  dashboardSidebar(
  sidebarMenu(
    
    menuItem("Overview",
             tabName = "overview",
             icon = icon("chart-bar")),
             
    menuItem("Player Stats",
              tabName = "players",
              icon = icon("users")),
             
    menuItem("Graphs",
              tabName = "graphs",
              icon =icon("chart-line"))
             
  )
  
),

dashboardBody(
  
  tabItems(
    
    tabItem(
      tabName = "overview",
      
      fluidRow(
        
        valueBoxOutput("avgPoints"),
        
        valueBoxOutput("avgRebounds"),
        
        valueBoxOutput("wins"
        )
      ),
      
      tabItem(
       tabName = "players",
      
       DTOutput("table")
      ),
      
      tabItem(
       tabName = "graphs",
      
       plotlyOutput("scatter"),
      
       plotlyOutput("bar")
      )
    )
  )
)


# Create the Server

server <- function(input,output) {
  output$avgPoints <- renderValueBox({
    valueBox(
      round(mean(basketball$points),1),
      "Average Points",
      codlor = "blue",
      icon =icon("basketball-ball")
    )
    
  })
  
  output$avgRebounds <- renderValueBox({
    
    valueBox(
      round(mean(basketball$Rebounds),1),
      "Averge Rebounds",
      color = "green"
    )
    
  })
  
  output$wins <- renderValueBox({
    
    valueBox(
      sum(basketball$Wins),
      "Total Wins",
      color = "orange"
    )
    
  })


# Create Interactive graphs

output$scatter <- renderPlotly({
  
  p <- ggplot(
    basketball,
    aes(rebounds, Points)) +
    geom_point(size = 3,
               color = "blue") +
    labs(title = "Points vs Rebounds")
  
  ggplotly(p)
  
})


# Bar chart

output$bar <- renderPlotly({
  
p <- ggplot(basketball,
            aes(Player, Points))+
  geom_col(fill - "steelblue")

ggplotly(p)

})


# Run the app

shinyApp(ui, server)


ui <- fluidPage(
  titlePanel("My Basketball Dashboard"),
  plotOutput("scatter")
)

server <- function(input,output, session) {
  
  output$scatter <- renderPlot({
    plot(1:10, 1:10)
  })
}

shinyApp(ui =ui, server = server)
